| Circular | Effective / Issue Date | Key Focus | OC Reference |

| **OC 141D – FY 2024‑25 Compliance Addendum to OC 141 series** | **April 15 2025** (deadline for compliance) | Further safeguards for UPI users; product‑level controls; extension of earlier OC 141 series | **OC 141D** |
| **OC 168 – Mandatory measures for Banks, PSPs & TPAPs** | **31 December 2023** | Implementation of mandatory actions; confidentiality of content | **OC 168** |
| **OC 169A – Addendum to Enhancement in UPI LITE Limits** | **31 August 2023** | Enhancing UPI LITE transaction limits and customer engagement | **OC 169A** |
| **OC 133A – Addendum to OC 133** | **28 February 2023** | Introduction of new initiation modes and channel identifiers for emerging use cases (IoT, embedded, voice‑based payments) | **OC 133A** |
| **OC 152 – Revised timelines on changes in UPI raw file** | **30 June 2022** | Updated deadlines for raw‑file changes | **OC 152** |

**Summary**

- The **most recent circular** is **OC 141D** (April 2025), which extends product‑level controls and sets a compliance deadline of 15 April 2025.  
- Earlier circulars that still apply are OC 168 (Dec 2023), OC 169A (Aug 2023), OC 133A (Feb 2023), and OC 152 (Jun 2022).  

*These details are drawn solely from the excerpts provided and should be verified against the official NPCI/URCS sources for the latest updates.*

user
Oc 177A explain
assistant

**OC 177A – Addendum to UPI Global Acceptance Limits and Country Code Enablement (FY 2026 27)**  

- **Purpose**: Revise the UPI Global Acceptance limits and expand the list of countries/currencies that can be enabled for UPI Global Acceptance.  
- **Revised Limits**  
  - *Europe (P2M)*: Per‑transaction and per‑day limits increased to **₹22 lakh**.  
  - *Other regions*: Limits remain as specified in the earlier circular **OC 177/2023‑24**.  
  - Each member bank may set its own limits within the overall limits set above.  
- **New Countries Added** (country code – currency code)  
  - Bahrain (BH – BHD)  
  - Cambodia (KH – KHR)  
  - Canada (CA – CAD)  
  - Kuwait (KW – KWD)  
  - New Zealand (NZ – NZD)  
  - Oman (OM – OMR)  
  - South Africa (ZA – ZAR)  
  - Switzerland (CH – CHF)  
- **Implementation**  
  - Issuer banks, PSP banks, and UPI applications must enable the new country/currency codes as needed.  
  - The changes take effect from **31 May 2026**.  
- **Compliance**  
  - Banks must continue to adhere to existing and future RBI, FEMA, and other relevant guidelines.  

*(This summary is drawn solely from the excerpts of OC 177A provided. Verify against the official NPCI/URCS circular for complete details.)*
OC 177A — UPI OC No 177A FY 2026 27 Addendum to UPI Global Acceptance Limits and Country Code Enablement
